values.value: Delivers as an in-app Custom Notification to specific Salesforce users - appears in the bell icon in the Lightning header and (if the user has the mobile app) as a push notification. No external service, no HTTP callout. Requires a Custom Notification Type record in the org that this notifier references.

## Salesforce Custom Notification setup
Custom Notifications are Salesforce's in-app notification framework. Users see them in the bell icon in the Lightning header, and on mobile via push notification if they have the Salesforce mobile app.
### One-time org setup
1. Setup -> **Custom Notifications** -> **New Custom Notification Type** 2. Name it something meaningful (e.g. `Nebula Logger Alert`) 3. Choose supported channels: **Desktop** and/or **Mobile** 4. Save. Note the **Developer Name** (used in the recipient's config JSON)
### Recipient setup
- Each recipient targets a specific **User** via `User` (lookup) - The recipient's config JSON must include the Custom Notification Type's developer name: ```json { "notificationTypeDeveloperName": "LoggerCustomNotification" } ``` - Recipients without `User` set are skipped (this notifier requires a target user)
### Customizing the title and body
- Optional `titleTemplate` and `bodyTemplate` string keys on the recipient's config JSON control the notification's title and body. Merge tokens use single braces: - `{RuleName}` - the notification rule's Name - `{FieldName__c}` - any field on the matching record (e.g. `{LoggingLevel__c}`, `{Message__c}`) - If `titleTemplate` is omitted, the default is `Nebula Logger: {RuleName}`. - If `bodyTemplate` is omitted, the body falls back to the matching record's Name (or its Id when the SObject has no Name field). - Tokens that don't resolve to a value on the current record render as an empty string.
### Limitations
- One notification per matching record. Use the rule's Deduplication Source Field to collapse duplicates before dispatch. - Text-only body (~90 characters visible on desktop, ~200 on mobile). No inline links, HTML, or markdown - the whole notification click-targets the matching record's Id via Salesforce's `setTargetId`. - No rich attachments, no threading, no delivery receipts beyond Salesforce's own retention.
